commit.message:
fix: use Start-BitsTransfer for IoW PBF download (browser-equivalent)

BITS (Background Intelligent Transfer Service) behaves identically to a
browser download and avoids Geofabrik's bot detection. Falls back to
curl.exe with full browser UA + Referer header. If both fail, prints
a clear manual download instruction.

commit.message:
feat: manual release workflow with tag input and changelog extraction (#36)

Replace workflow_run trigger with workflow_dispatch that takes a tag
name (e.g. v1.2.1). The workflow extracts the matching changelog
section from CHANGELOG.md and uses it as the release body. If no
changelog section is found, falls back to auto-generated notes.

Also adds a frontend build job (was previously downloaded from CI run
artifacts which are not available in manual dispatch).

commit.message:
Rewrite Dropout and LayerNorm as from-scratch C++ implementations

Dropout:
- Replace math-block in Section 1 with SVG figure showing
  input → mask → output visually (training vs inference)
- Replace torch::dropout wrapper with from-scratch implementation
  using torch::bernoulli mask + scaling
- Update argument table to logic-step table
- Add callout pointing to torch::dropout as library shortcut
- Update TransformerBlock code snippets to use our dropout()

LayerNorm:
- Replace torch::nn::LayerNorm usage with from-scratch
  LayerNormImpl struct (register_parameter for gamma/beta,
  manual mean/var/normalize/scale in forward)
- Update Constructor section to show our two params (n_embd, eps)
- Update Forward Pass to show annotated step-by-step code
- Add callout pointing to torch::nn::LayerNorm as library shortcut

commit.message:
fix: bound large file primitive hashing

Keep full-file SHA-256 for normal files, but switch oversized file primitive hashing to a bounded prefix SHA-256 with explicit metadata describing the hashing mode and bytes hashed.

Reuse the same guardrail for file-backed content digest fallback and add oversized-file tests for both metadata collection and digest behavior.

commit.message:
fix: use track-local format for PCM padding, add reconnect jitter

- Frame padding now uses the AudioFormat captured at stream start
  (prebuffer.output_format or a snapshot of _normalized_params)
  instead of self._normalized_format which may change mid-stream.
- Reconnect backoff now includes ±20% random jitter to prevent
  thundering-herd reconnects on outages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve teams-bot-infra skill: optional endpoint, auto .env, and error handling (#80)

* Fix Azure CLI detection on Windows using cmd /c wrapper

Root cause: execFile() cannot spawn .cmd files directly on Windows,
resulting in spawn EINVAL error. Previous attempts used shell:true
which introduced command injection vulnerabilities.

Solution: Use cmd.exe with /c flag to execute az.cmd on Windows,
which is secure (blocks injection) and cross-platform compatible.

- Windows: cmd /c az.cmd <args>
- Unix: az <args>

Security: Verified that special characters (&&, ||, ;) are treated
as literal arguments, not executed as shell commands.

Fixes #28

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add teams-bot-infra agent skill for Teams bot infrastructure management

Implement production-grade agent skill following the 5-phase development lifecycle:
- Phase 1: Discovered and validated workflow via manual execution
- Phase 2: Designed modular architecture (single skill covering create, configure, troubleshoot)
- Phase 3: Implemented SKILL.md with verification checkpoints and error recovery
- Phase 4: Validated against real workflow (leveraged Phase 1 testing)
- Phase 5: Packaged for distribution with installation documentation

Skill features:
- Complete bot creation workflow (CLI install → auth → create → verify)
- Verification checkpoints requiring evidence at each step
- Common operations (update endpoint, view apps, update CLI)
- Error recovery (auth required, sideload disabled)
- Resource links (Teams SDK, devtunnels, Microsoft docs)

Scope: Bot infrastructure only (excludes bot code development and hosting)

Installation: npx skills add heyitsaamir/teamscli --skill teams-bot-infra

Resolves #37

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: improve wording in teams-bot-infra skill description

Change "guides you through" to "will guide you in" for better clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve teams-bot-infra skill: optional endpoint, auto .env, and error handling

- Make messaging endpoint optional for proactive-only bots
  - Add proactive flows explanation and warning
  - Note that endpoint can be added later via teams app edit
- Change credential handling to auto-write to .env file by default
  - Prompt user for existing .env or create new one
  - Safely append/update without overwriting other values
- Add "This app cannot be found" error recovery
  - Explain cross-tenant installation issue
  - Common scenario: M365 Developer Program vs work tenant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

commit.message:
feat(generator): format CREATE TABLE with aligned columns and own-line paren (#27)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Implements the `CREATE TABLE` formatting and lowercase data type rules
tracked in #26.

### Changes

**`src/jarify/generator.py`**

- `schema_sql` — puts the opening `(` on its own line when in pretty
mode, consistent with the existing CTE layout rule.
- `schema_columns_sql` — splits column definitions from table-level
constraints, computes column-name and type alignment widths, renders
columns padded to align all type and constraint tokens, and inserts a
blank line before table-level constraints (`PRIMARY KEY`, `UNIQUE`,
`CHECK`, etc.) when present.
- `columndef_sql` — honours the two alignment widths stored during
`schema_columns_sql`; falls back to default behaviour outside `CREATE
TABLE` (e.g. `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`).
- `datatype_sql` — lowercases all data type names (`text`, `int`,
`timestamp`, `decimal`, etc.) while leaving SQL statement keywords
(`SELECT`, `FROM`, `NOT NULL`, `DEFAULT`, `PRIMARY KEY`, etc.)
uppercase.

**`tests/fixtures/create_table/`**

Three new fixture pairs:

| Fixture | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `basic` | Full DDL: mixed custom/built-in types, `DEFAULT`, nullable
columns, table-level `PRIMARY KEY` |
| `simple_no_constraints` | Plain table with no constraints; verifies no
blank line is added |
| `inline_primary_key` | Column-level `PRIMARY KEY` constraint; verifies
no blank line is added |

**`tests/fixtures/duckdb/type_cast.expected.sql`** — updated to
lowercase types (`int`, `text`, `boolean`).

**`docs/sql-style-guide.md`** — adds `CREATE TABLE` layout rule and
updates the Keywords section to document the keyword-uppercase /
type-lowercase split.

Resolves #26

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: RFC 9535 compliant JSONPath service with sort extension functions (#129)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement RFC 9535 compliant JsonPathService

- Add IJsonPathService interface in Core/Abstractions
- Add JsonPathService with full RFC 9535 support:
  - Name selectors (dot notation and bracket notation with quoted strings)
  - Index selectors (including negative indices)
  - Wildcard selectors ($.*  and $[*])
  - Slice selectors ($[1:3], $[::2], $[1:5:2])
  - Descendant segments ($..foo, $..[*])
  - Multiple selectors in one bracket ($[0,2,'name'])
  - Filter selectors with logical/comparison expressions
  - RFC 9535 built-in functions (length, count, match, search, value)
- Refactor SyntaxPathFilter to delegate to JsonPathService
- Register IJsonPathService in App.axaml.cs DI
- Add 78 comprehensive tests covering all RFC 9535 features

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/e00a6310-abf7-46b7-be8e-ce07beb24bec

Co-authored-by: callsmith <267777561+callsmith@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove static service instantiation from SyntaxPathFilter

- SyntaxPathFilter.TryTransform now accepts IJsonPathService as parameter
- FilteredSyntaxViewer exposes JsonPath property (injectable, defaults to new JsonPathService)
- Update SyntaxPathFilterTests to pass service explicitly
- Improve test assertions for DescendantIndex and ExistenceTestOnObject tests

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/e00a6310-abf7-46b7-be8e-ce07beb24bec

Co-authored-by: callsmith <267777561+callsmith@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add sort/sort_asc/sort_desc JSONPath extension functions

- Add IJsonPathService.TryQuery() for runtime error propagation
- Introduce IPathStep interface with SortStep record alongside Segment
- Parser detects .sort(), .sort_asc(), .sort_desc() function calls
  - sort()/sort_asc() ascending; sort_desc() descending
  - Sort expression (property name) required for object arrays
  - Sort expression forbidden for primitive arrays
  - Descendant sort ($..sort_asc()) is a parse error
- Numeric, string and mixed-type comparison via SortKeyComparer (nulls last)
- SyntaxPathFilter uses TryQuery to surface runtime sort errors in filter bar
- 18 new tests covering all sort behaviors and error cases

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/b91a668d-bd15-49e8-bde5-f3aa3fbf66bd

Adds a small circular '?' badge next to the JSONPATH FILTER label.
Hovering it shows a tooltip that explains RFC 9535 compliance and
the sort extension functions with usage examples.
Badge is only visible when the language is JSON.

Layout now reads "JSONPATH FILTER ?:  Active" — the ? badge

requested placement.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/50ca3ad1-708e-41b0-b0e7-bd53803ff8f0

Co-authored-by: callsmith <267777561+callsmith@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: sort after wildcard/slice selectors flattens then sorts correctly

When [*] or a slice selector expands an array into individual elements,
the sort step now detects it's in "flat-list mode" (no node is itself an
array) and treats the whole nodes list as one virtual array to sort.

Paths like $..items[*].sort(price) and $.items[0:3].sort(v) now work.

- Refactored TryApplySort: extracted TrySortElements helper
- Added flat-list detection via TrueForAll(n => n.ValueKind != Array)
- Updated TryQuery_Sort_OnNonArray error expectation (now "expression")
- Added 5 new sort-after-wildcard/slice/descendant tests

commit.message:
feat: rewrite CSP headers instead of stripping them

Content-Security-Policy headers are now rewritten on the fly to replace
upstream domain references with the proxy's own address, rather than being
stripped entirely.

Rewriting rules:
  - https://target.com        → http://localhost:PORT
  - https://*.target.com      → http://localhost:PORT  (wildcard; all subdomains
                                  served from same proxy origin via /__sd__/)
  - wss://target.com          → ws://localhost:PORT    (WebSocket downgrade)
  - *.target.com              → localhost:PORT
  - target.com                → localhost:PORT
  - keywords ('self', nonce, sha256, unsafe-*) → unchanged
  - report-uri / report-to directives → stripped (prevent leaking to upstream)

localhost:PORT is fully valid as a CSP host-source. Since all proxied content
(including subdomains via /__sd__/) is served from the same proxy origin, a
single localhost:PORT entry covers all subdomain resources.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Output bit depth: Auto/16-bit/24-bit

commit.message:
fix: statically link lzma to avoid codesign Team ID mismatch

hive-daemon was dynamically linking to Homebrew's liblzma.5.dylib via
the xz2 crate. After codesigning, macOS dyld rejects the library due
to mismatched Team IDs, causing the daemon to crash-loop on launch.

Adding lzma-sys with the 'static' feature compiles lzma from source
and embeds it in the binary, eliminating the runtime dependency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[WIP] Fix httpx AsyncClient resource leak in BotApplication (#81)

* Initial plan

* fix: close httpx AsyncClient to prevent resource leak

- Add aclose() method to ConversationClient to close underlying HTTP client
- Add aclose() and async context manager support to BotApplication
- Update BotApp (FastAPI) to use lifespan hook for automatic cleanup
- Add tests for resource cleanup and context manager support
- Update FastAPI sample to demonstrate proper cleanup pattern

Fixes resource leak where httpx AsyncClient was never closed, causing
TCP connections, file descriptors, and memory to not be released in
long-running servers.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/rido-min/botas/sessions/6d7186cc-dded-4734-b8c1-be840de5dfbc

commit.message:
fix(enrichment): always rank all immediate_attention matches

Previously the top-50 budget cap excluded ~162 immediate_attention matches,
so they never got snapshots and were invisible in the matches list.

Changes:
- compute_enrichment_budget_rank: split into two passes — ALL immediate_attention
  matches are ranked first (no cap), then remaining budget slots (limit=50) are
  filled with lower-priority matches (new/defensive_gap/watchlist/stale)
- _fetch_ranked_matches / enrichment_worker: increase fetch limit to 500 to
  accommodate all immediate_attention ranks
- Dashboard card (233) and matches list will now agree once next enrichment
  cycle processes all ranked matches

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
